author: Dr. Lizard
title: What hemoglobin improvement do proximal complement inhibitors achieve compared to C5 inhibitors?
answer: Both pegcetacoplan and iptacopan increase hemoglobin by approximately 2.4 grams per deciliter versus C5 inhibitors.
detail: Proximal inhibitors control both intravascular and extravascular hemolysis, unlike C5 inhibitors that only block intravascular lysis.
Many patients achieve hemoglobin normalization above 12 grams per deciliter, eliminating transfusion dependence completely.
C5 inhibitors stabilize hemoglobin in only about half of patients due to ongoing C3-mediated extravascular hemolysis.
The 2.4 gram per deciliter improvement translates to significant quality of life gains and reduced fatigue.
releaseNote: Proximal complement inhibitors, such as pegcetacoplan and iptacopan, offer improved hemoglobin improvement compared to C5 inhibitors, with significant quality of life gains and reduced fatigue.
